{{Quote|The sick and injured will always have a healer to salve their wounds, but to whom does the healer go?|Bahm Gilyad|<ref name="MedStar I" />}}
'''Bahm Gilyad''' was a [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] who lived during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. During the war, he formalized the rules and responsibilities of the medical profession, which included ideas for doctors to maintain themselves mentally and physically. His role in formalizing the medical profession was still known by the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a conflict fought between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] that occurred nearly five thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the Great Hyperspace War.

==Biography==
A [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]], Bahm Gilyad was alive during the [[Time/Legends|time]]<ref name="MedStar I" /> of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]],<ref name="Date">''[[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons]]'' states that Gilyad lived during the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspce Conflict]], which the book states to occur 5,000 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] earlier than the story's [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]] date. However, the Stark Hyperspace Conflict occurred in [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], according to ''[[Star Wars: The Stark Hyperspace War]]''. Since ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' confirms that Gilyad lived 5,000 years before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], this article assumes that the information given in ''MedStar I: Battle Surgeons'' about the Stark Hyperspace Conflict was in error and that Gilyad was alive around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], which occurred in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> which was fought between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> During the conflict,<ref name="Date" /> Gilyad formalized the rules and responsibilities of the medical profession,<ref name="MedStar I" /> which included many of his ideas on maintaining a physician's health, both physically and mentally. He documented his rules for practicing medicine in a form that survived for many [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]].<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I|342|Gilyad Bahm}}</ref> In [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> while a [[Battle of Drongar|battle]] raged on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] that raged between the Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], the Galactic Republic surgeon [[Jos Vondar]] thought about a quote by Gilyad when listening to his friend [[Zan Yant]] play the [[quetarra]] while stationed at [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit/Legends|Republic Mobile Surgical Unit]] [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7|7]].<ref name="MedStar I" />

==Personality and traits==
As a healer, Bahm Gilyad was influential enough to establish rules for practicing medicine that were still used nearly five thousand years later. He also thought in detail about how physicians should maintain their mental and physical health.<ref name="TCSWE" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bahm Gilyad was first mentioned in ''[[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons]]'', a [[2004]] [[Clone Wars multimedia project|''Clone Wars'']] novel written by [[Michael Reaves]] and [[Steve Perry]] as the first book in the [[MedStar Duology|''MedStar'' duology]]. The novel mentions that Gilyad lived five thousand years before the events of the book during the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace Conflict]],<ref name="MedStar I" /> but the conflict that occurred around that date was known as the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].<ref name="NEC" /> As Bahm Gilyad's [[2008]] entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' mentions that he lived five thousand years before the Clone Wars,<ref name="TCSWE" /> this article treats the mention of the Stark Hyperspace Conflict as an error.

*''[[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons]]'' {{1stm}}
